san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the proceedings. The city offered multiple ways to tune in, including cable TV, online streaming, and phone access, plus live translations in over 50 languages for residents who need them.
keyTopics: Live translations available in 50+ languages via Wordly
Multiple viewing options: Cable Channel 26, YouTube, Zoom, and phone call-in
Meeting accessible online at sanjoseca.gov and YouTube
No public comment period during this session

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026 has been cancelled. The next zoning hearing will take place on April 8, 2026 instead. If you had plans to discuss zoning issues with the city, you'll need to wait until the April date.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning matters affected

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that funds affordable housing and community development programs. The commission approved previous meeting minutes and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to help residents with housing and related services.
keyTopics: Federal housing grant applications being reviewed for funding
Affordable housing and community development initiatives
Approval of February meeting minutes
Community organizations presenting funding requests

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and thrive. They discussed ways to reach out to shops at local shopping centers, promote a summer campaign to get residents shopping locally, and offer incentives like tax breaks or support for small businesses and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get properly licensed
Summer 'Shop Local' campaign to boost local retailers
Partnerships with private companies to sponsor city events
Financial incentives for small businesses and startups
Direct outreach to shopping centers and business owners

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting where they postponed several items for later discussion. They also approved a plan to deal with overgrown weeds in the city as part of the county's annual weed control program, and opened up public comment on how federal housing grant money should be spent in the city next year.
keyTopics: Multiple agenda items delayed to future meetings
City weed abatement program for 2026
Federal housing grant funding plans for 2026-27

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to approve several routine city matters. They recognized March as Women's History Month, approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector for email security software (Mimecast) costing about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross facility agreement for disaster response
Email security software contract ($93,383 annual cost)
